reasoning: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.

concerns.analysis: The source document contains 46 pages. The conversion result explicitly states that 35 sections were omitted for token budget. Major portions of the document are missing, including pages 6-8, 10-33, and 35-42. This constitutes a massive loss of core information.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: 46 pages of content including FCC Fee Diversion Background, NG911 Program Areas, Glossary, and WA 911 Jobs marketing plans.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: There are several text fidelity issues. First, in House Bill 203 (Page 34), the output loses the semantic meaning of the amendment by failing to mark '2021' as a strikethrough and '2022' as an underline, making the change in the law ambiguous (Major). Second, there are minor truncations in table data, such as 'MONDAY 10-1' instead of 'MONDAY 10-11P' (Page 43). Finally, extra characters like asterisks were added to the agenda items (Minor).
concerns.location: House Bill 203 (element 446) and KOMO Contract Table (element 562)

concerns.analysis: While most images (logos, banner, analytics chart) are present, significant meaningful diagrams from page 38 of the source PDF are missing in the HTML output. Specifically, the 'NC 911 Board NG9-1-1 Migration Process' flowchart and the 'NG9-1-1 Call Handling Solution Architecture' network diagram are absent, with only their text headings remaining. These are critical technical diagrams required for understanding the document content.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 38 of the PDF contains two meaningful diagrams: 'NC 911 Board NG9-1-1 Migration Process' (a circular flowchart) and 'NG9-1-1 Call Handling Solution Architecture' (a detailed network diagram).
concerns.output_quote: In the HTML screenshot, these sections contain only the text headings, and the corresponding diagrams are completely missing from the rendered content.
concerns.location: Page 38, 'NC 911 Board NG9-1-1 Migration Process' and 'NG9-1-1 Call Handling Solution Architecture' sections.
